scenario: Zenith Bank is reviewing a Camunda 8.8 DMN decision table that handles 6000 loans per week through a credit-scoring decision for the bank. The team is documenting which attribute on the input XML element carries the short description shown on each column header, and whether the docs page treats that attribute as required or optional.
question: Which attribute carries the short description of a DMN input column, and how does the docs page describe whether it is required?

options.text: The id attribute on the input element carries the description and the docs require it on every input clause
The typeRef attribute on the inputExpression element carries the description and the docs require it for inputs
The text body inside the inputExpression element carries the description and the docs require it for inputs
The label attribute on the input element carries the description and the docs say it is optional but recommended

a.text: Incorrect. The id attribute is the unique input identifier and is required, but it is not the short description; the short description is carried by the separate label attribute on the input element.

b.text: Incorrect. The typeRef attribute on the inputExpression child element declares the input's data type rather than its short description; the description is carried by the input element's label attribute.

c.text: Incorrect. The text inside the inputExpression element holds the FEEL expression body that produces the input value rather than a description; the description is carried by the input element's label attribute.

d.text: Correct. The DMN docs describe the input label as a short description of the input set on the input XML element via the label attribute, and they note that the label is not required but is recommended because it helps to understand the decision.

explanation: An input label is a short description set on the input XML element through the label attribute. The docs note that this label is not required but is recommended because it improves readability of the decision table for its readers.
